description: This figure presents TEM analyses of deformation-induced microstructures in an Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel at 6% (a-d) and 12% (e-h) engineering strains. Bright-field images (a, e) reveal increasing dislocation densities in austenite and B2 phases with strain, showing nonplanar slip bands, dislocation pile-ups at phase boundaries, and bowing-out configurations at 12%. Dark-field images (b, f) with SAED patterns (insets) demonstrate weakening and volume fraction reduction of L'12 ordered domains within slip bands, evidenced by attenuated [011] superlattice reflections. Inverse FFT images (c, g) confirm the progressive dissolution of LRO domains via dislocation shearing. Images (d, h) illustrate VC nanoprecipitates impeding dislocation motion, with hindering effects becoming more pronounced at higher strains. The figure evidences that dual-nanoprecipitation strengthens the steel through shearable LRO domains (providing dynamic precipitation strengthening) and non-shearable VC carbides (Orowan strengthening), while phase boundaries accumulate geometrically necessary dislocations for HDI hardening.
